content: Scope creep patterns in logistics MIS: nearly 50% of projects experience feature creep (PMI data). Logistics-specific patterns include adding delivery tracking after it was explicitly deferred, adding financial/billing modules when focus should be operational, adding multi-warehouse support mid-build, adding customer portal functionality for what should be an internal tool, and over-engineering reports (pivot tables, Excel exports) before core CRUD is stable. Prevention: maintain a written out-of-scope list, require a scope change to be formally approved before any new feature enters the backlog, time-box phases to prevent perpetual design.
